title: Bank of Japan raises interest rate to 1%, highest since 1995
summary: The central bank seeks to control inflation and the weak yen, amid a market that celebrates with records but worries about rising mortgages.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: Magnitude 5.5 earthquake shakes Kanto region, leaves minor damage
summary: The quake, with epicenter in Gunma and Saitama, reached an intensity of 5 on the Japanese scale and caused temporary suspension of bullet trains, with no serious casualties reported.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: G7 summit debates Ukraine, Middle East, and cooperation on critical minerals
summary: G7 leaders, including Prime Minister Takaichi, address energy security in the Gulf and military threats from China, Russia, and North Korea.

title: United States and Iran sign memorandum to end hostilities
summary: The agreement paves the way for formal negotiations, while Israel is skeptical and betting markets react with volatility.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: Emperor Naruhito continues official visit to the Netherlands
summary: The Japanese monarch strengthens bilateral ties with cultural and diplomatic activities in Amsterdam.

title: Seibu Lions win Japanese interleague baseball championship for first time
summary: The Pacific League team won the title with outstanding defense and solid collective pitching, winning in a close game.

title: FBI foils planned attack on an event with Donald Trump
summary: Several people were arrested for allegedly planning an attack during a martial arts tournament at the White House.

title: NHK's reception fee income falls for first time in six years
summary: The reduction in the number of delinquent households is attributed to the stricter collection measures implemented by the public broadcaster.

title: Japanese parliament advances law penalizing destruction of national flag
summary: Four parties are pushing legislation that criminalizes damage to the flag of Japan, amid a debate on freedom of expression.

title: Japan faces increase in bears in urban areas, Tokyo considers resuming hunting
summary: Authorities seek measures to control the bear population after multiple sightings and attacks on people in residential areas.

title: Iranian national football team sparks controversies at 2026 World Cup
summary: While some fans wave protest flags, the team faces restrictions in the United States and generates debate in its country.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: Japanese government proposes reducing consumption tax by 1% from April 2025
summary: An expert committee presents a plan to ease the tax burden on households, although political debate on its implementation continues.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi announces start of negotiations for trade agreement with South America
summary: Japan seeks to strengthen economic ties with the South American region through a comprehensive economic partnership agreement.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: Defense ministers of Japan and the Netherlands reaffirm military cooperation
summary: The meeting takes place in the context of global tensions and prior attendance at a World Cup soccer match.

title: Prime minister attends pre-gala of Japan Music Awards
summary: The event seeks to boost the Japanese music industry with a commercial focus and the presence of high-ranking officials.

title: Number of bookstores in Japan falls below 10,000 for the first time
summary: Digitalization and changes in reading habits lead to a continuous decline of physical bookstores in the country.

title: Fujitsu president resigns after scandal involving inappropriate conduct toward a woman
summary: The Japanese technology company faces a leadership crisis while investigating the incident that led to the resignation of its top executive.

title: Eight people die in crash of B-52 bomber during test mission in California
summary: The accident occurred in a desert area and authorities are investigating the causes of the crash, one of the worst in years.

title: Shohei Ohtani leads early voting for MLB All-Star Game
summary: The Japanese player for the Los Angeles Angels tops the vote list as a fan favorite.

title: Osaka University hires people with hikikomori syndrome as researchers in virtual environments
summary: The project allows participants to work anonymously in virtual worlds, promoting their social and labor inclusion.
